content.problemStatement:
Given a string SSS of length NNN containing only numeric characters, find the number of **Nice Pairs**.

A **Nice Pair** is a pair of indices - (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N and j−i=∣Sj−Si∣j - i = |S_j - S_i|j−i=∣Sj​−Si​∣.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains a single integer, NNN, the length of the string
The second line of each testcase contains a string SSS of length NNN. The string contains only numeric characters [0−9][0-9][0−9]


Output Format
For each testcase, output a single integer - the number of nice pairs in the given string.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of digits '0'‑'9'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of nice pairs in the given string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: all three possible pairs satisfy the condition.\nTest case 2: only pairs (2,3) and (3,5) are nice.\nTest case 3: there are nine pairs that meet j-i = |S_j - S_i|.

solution.approach: For a pair (i, j) with i < j we need j - i = |S[j] - S[i]|. The left side is the index distance, the right side is at most 9 because digits are 0‑9. Therefore for each position i we only have to check at most 9 possible j: j = i + d for d = 1..9. If j is inside the string and |digit_j - digit_i| equals d, the pair is nice. Summing over all i gives the answer. This runs in O(N * 9) = O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra memory.
